full_name: Ministry of Indigenous Relations and Reconciliation
blurb: Leads B.C.'s pursuit of reconciliation with First Nations and Indigenous Peoples — implementing the Declaration on the Rights of Indigenous Peoples Act, negotiating treaties and reconciliation agreements, and supporting Indigenous economic development, communities, women and languages.

goals.title: GOAL 1
goals.short_name: British Columbia's laws and relationships with Indigenous Peoples reflect the Declaration Act
goals.description: The ministry leads B.C.'s participation in treaty and reconciliation negotiations and guides the cross-government implementation of the Declaration on the Rights of Indigenous Peoples Act.

main_services.name: Implement the Declaration on the Rights of Indigenous Peoples Act

supporting.name: Track progress on Declaration Act implementation
supporting.desc: Annual reports and the five-year Declaration Act Action Plan track the Province's progress aligning provincial laws with the UN Declaration and meeting reconciliation commitments.

channels.label: Declaration on the Rights of Indigenous Peoples Act

legislation.full_name: Declaration on the Rights of Indigenous Peoples Act - Alignment of laws, action plan and annual reporting

mandate_tags.tip: Lead work to review and update the Declaration Act Action Plan by 2027, for the next five-year period

supporting.name: Enter into a shared decision-making agreement with the Province
supporting.desc: Sections 6 and 7 of the Declaration Act give the Province flexibility to enter into agreements with a broader range of Indigenous governing bodies and to exercise statutory decision-making authority together.

legislation.full_name: Declaration on the Rights of Indigenous Peoples Act - Sections 6-7, agreements and joint decision-making

main_services.name: Negotiate a treaty or reconciliation agreement

supporting.name: Negotiate a modern treaty with the Province and Canada
supporting.desc: The ministry leads B.C.'s participation in Final Agreement and Agreement-in-Principle negotiations with First Nations and Canada, facilitated by the independent BC Treaty Commission's six-stage negotiations framework.
supporting.users: First Nations in the treaty process

channels.label: Ministry of Indigenous Relations and Reconciliation

mandate_tags.tip: Given the strains of conflict over boundaries, and the legal costs and uncertainty associated with unclear boundaries, work with First Nations partners to prioritize Action 1.1 of the Declaration Act Action Plan relating to overlap and boundary dispute resolution

supporting.name: Negotiate a reconciliation agreement or other arrangement with the Province
supporting.desc: The Province negotiates reconciliation agreements, accords, memorandums of understanding and other arrangements with First Nations outside the formal treaty process to close socio-economic gaps and create economic certainty.

mandate_tags.tip: Build partnerships that advance reconciliation in measurable ways and create tangible benefits for First Nations and all British Columbians, such as building more affordable housing, generating clean energy, creating good jobs and protecting our environment

main_services.name: Get support for consultation on land and resource decisions

supporting.name: Get government support for consultation on decisions affecting your territory
supporting.desc: Guidance and procedure manuals help government officials and project proponents meet legal obligations to consult and accommodate First Nations before decisions affecting Aboriginal rights, title or treaty rights.

supporting.name: Find your local First Nations consultation contacts
supporting.desc: An interactive online mapping tool identifies First Nation contacts and consultation areas so the public, industry, other governments and First Nations can identify who to talk to about a proposed activity.

goals.title: GOAL 2
goals.short_name: Indigenous communities and businesses share in economic growth and reconciliation
goals.description: The ministry supports Indigenous entrepreneurs and communities through business financing, capacity-building funds, and partnerships that create tangible economic and social benefits.

supporting.name: Apply for an Indigenous business loan
supporting.desc: The First Citizens Fund Business Loan Program provides business loans to Indigenous entrepreneurs, delivered by All Nations Trust Company in partnership with four Aboriginal Capital Corporations across the province.

channels.label: First Citizens Fund Business Loan Program

supporting.name: Get help starting or growing your Indigenous business
supporting.desc: Indigenous Business Resources and regional Indigenous Business Advisory Centres in Cranbrook, Fort St. John and Prince George help entrepreneurs with business start-up, expansion, partnerships and planning.

supporting.name: Apply for Indigenous economic and community development funding
supporting.desc: The independent New Relationship Trust invests a $100-million endowment in First Nations economic development, governance capacity, education, language and culture, and Elders and youth initiatives.

mandate_tags.tip: Ensure that at the end of this term, people living in Indigenous communities in the province have seen an improvement in their quality of life through our government's interventions

supporting.name: Apply for First Citizens Fund cultural, educational or economic funding
supporting.desc: The First Citizens Fund, a perpetual fund created in 1969, uses investment earnings to support cultural, educational and economic development initiatives and organizations serving Indigenous people in B.C.

main_services.name: Access community and cultural support programs

supporting.name: Find Indigenous organizations and services in your area
supporting.desc: The Guide to Indigenous Organization and Services lists more than 1,100 Indigenous, First Nations and Métis organizations offering culture and language, business, child and family, education, employment, health, housing and legal services.
supporting.users: General public, Indigenous people and communities

channels.label: Guide to Indigenous Organization and Services

supporting.name: Get support after the discovery of unmarked graves or a residential school site
supporting.desc: A First Nations liaison and mental health, wellness and cultural supports help lead communities investigate, protect and commemorate the sites of former Indian Residential Schools and Indian Hospitals in B.C.
supporting.users: First Nations and Tribal Councils with a former residential school or hospital site

supporting.name: Get support as an urban or off-reserve Indigenous person
supporting.desc: The Off-Reserve Aboriginal Action Plan and Friendship Centres Program support better education and job training, healthier family life, and strengthened cultures and traditions for the 78% of Indigenous people living, studying and working in urban and off-reserve areas.

goals.title: GOAL 3
goals.short_name: Indigenous women, languages and cultures are respected and represented
goals.description: The ministry supports Indigenous women's leadership and the revitalization of First Nations languages and cultures across the province.

main_services.name: Get advice and representation as an Indigenous woman

supporting.name: Take part in the Minister's Advisory Council on Indigenous Women
supporting.desc: MACIW provides advice to government on how to improve the quality of life for Indigenous women in B.C., guided by Indigenous worldviews, and reports to the Minister of Indigenous Relations and Reconciliation.

channels.label: Minister's Advisory Council on Indigenous Women (MACIW)

main_services.name: Protect and revitalize Indigenous languages and culture

supporting.name: Access Indigenous language and culture revitalization supports
supporting.desc: The Province funds the First Peoples' Cultural Council and Declaration Act Action Plan commitments to protect and revitalize B.C.'s 35 First Nation languages and Indigenous cultures and heritage.

foundational_services.name: Lead cross-government implementation of the Declaration Act
foundational_services.desc: The Declaration Act Secretariat, a central agency established in 2022, guides and supports implementation of all sections of the Declaration Act across B.C. government, including alignment of provincial laws with the UN Declaration.

legislation.full_name: Declaration on the Rights of Indigenous Peoples Act - Sections 3-5, alignment of laws and action plan

mandate_tags.tip: Through the Declaration Act Secretariat, lead work with the Attorney General to ensure alignment of BC's laws with the rights of Indigenous peoples

foundational_services.name: Coordinate B.C.'s participation in treaty and reconciliation agreement negotiations
foundational_services.desc: The ministry leads the Province's participation in treaty negotiations, interim measures and other agreements with First Nations and the federal government on lands, resources, governance, fiscal relations and capacity-building, and coordinates cross-government implementation once concluded.

mandate_tags.tip: Continue work with First Nations partners, local governments and agencies to implement treaties and other agreements

foundational_services.name: Provide advice and guidance on First Nations consultation to government agencies and proponents
foundational_services.desc: The ministry provides advice and guidance in First Nations consultation to government decision-makers and produces resources that help proponents meet their consultation and accommodation obligations.

foundational_services.name: Administer the First Citizens Fund
foundational_services.desc: The ministry administers the First Citizens Fund, a perpetual fund created in 1969 under the Special Accounts Appropriation and Control Act, whose principal is held as capital and whose investment earnings fund cultural, educational and economic initiatives for Indigenous people in B.C.

foundational_services.name: Support the Indigenous Business and Investment Council
foundational_services.desc: The ministry supports the Indigenous Business and Investment Council, which develops strategies to increase Indigenous participation in the economy and promote investment models between Indigenous people and the private sector.

foundational_services.name: Review ministry programs and funding for efficiency and relevance
foundational_services.desc: Ongoing review, with the Ministry of Finance, of all ministry programs and initiatives to ensure they remain relevant, efficient and deliver improved outcomes for Indigenous peoples within current budget constraints.

mandate_tags.tip: Work with the Minister of Finance to review all existing ministry programs and initiatives to ensure programs remain relevant, are efficient, grow the economy, and deliver an improved quality of life for Indigenous peoples in the province

foundational_services.name: Support work to address anti-Indigenous racism
foundational_services.desc: The ministry works with the Parliamentary Secretary for Anti-Racism Initiatives on cross-government efforts to address anti-Indigenous racism.

mandate_tags.tip: Work with the Parliamentary Secretary for Anti-Racism Initiatives on anti-Indigenous racism

foundational_services.name: Coordinate Crown land transfers for First Nations economic development
foundational_services.desc: In cooperation with the Ministry of Water, Land and Resource Stewardship, the ministry develops and implements approaches to accelerate agreed-upon Crown land transfers that support First Nation community and economic development.

mandate_tags.tip: In cooperation with the Minister of Water, Land and Resource Stewardship, continue to develop and implement approaches to accelerate agreed-upon land transfers that enhance First Nation community and economic development

cross_cutting: Growing the economy and creating jobs

mandate_legend.text: In order to protect key services that British Columbians rely on, work with the Minister of Finance to review all existing ministry programs and initiatives to ensure programs remain relevant, are efficient, grow the economy, and deliver an improved quality of life for Indigenous peoples in the province
mandate_legend.mapping: -> Foundational: review ministry programs and funding for efficiency and relevance

mandate_legend.text: Build partnerships that advance reconciliation in measurable ways and create tangible benefits for First Nations and all British Columbians, such as building more affordable housing, generating clean energy, creating good jobs and protecting our environment
mandate_legend.mapping: -> Negotiate a reconciliation agreement or other arrangement with the Province

mandate_legend.text: Ensure that at the end of this term, people living in Indigenous communities in the province have seen an improvement in their quality of life through our government's interventions by prioritizing initiatives that make a meaningful difference in the lives of Indigenous peoples in this province
mandate_legend.mapping: -> Apply for Indigenous economic and community development funding

mandate_legend.text: Continue work with First Nations partners, local governments and agencies to implement treaties and other agreements
mandate_legend.mapping: -> Foundational: coordinate B.C.'s participation in treaty and reconciliation agreement negotiations

mandate_legend.text: Given the strains of conflict over boundaries, and the legal costs and uncertainty associated with unclear boundaries, work with First Nations partners to prioritize Action 1.1 of the Declaration Act Action Plan relating to overlap and boundary dispute resolution
mandate_legend.mapping: -> Negotiate a modern treaty with the Province and Canada

mandate_legend.text: Through the Declaration Act Secretariat, lead work with the Attorney General to ensure alignment of BC's laws with the rights of Indigenous peoples
mandate_legend.mapping: -> Foundational: lead cross-government implementation of the Declaration Act

mandate_legend.text: Lead work to review and update the Declaration Act Action Plan by 2027, for the next five-year period
mandate_legend.mapping: -> Track progress on Declaration Act implementation

mandate_legend.text: Work with the Parliamentary Secretary for Anti-Racism Initiatives on anti-Indigenous racism
mandate_legend.mapping: -> Foundational: support work to address anti-Indigenous racism

mandate_legend.text: In cooperation with the Minister of Water, Land and Resource Stewardship, continue to develop and implement approaches to accelerate agreed-upon land transfers that enhance First Nation community and economic development
mandate_legend.mapping: -> Foundational: coordinate Crown land transfers for First Nations economic development
